Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper describes cis-OPDA as a signaling molecule and a homeostasis target during stress responses.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show recombinant ILR1 hydrolyzes OPDA-aa to release cis-OPDA, and ilr1-containing mutant backgrounds accumulate more OPDA-aa after wounding, consistent with a role in OPDA-aa turnover and cis-OPDA homeostasis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements match the Results/Discussion: ILR1 hydrolyzed all six tested OPDA-aa with preference for Ala/Asp/Glu; ilr1-containing mutants accumulated OPDA-Ala and OPDA-Phe after wounding; the authors conclude ILR1 redundantly contributes to OPDA-aa hydrolysis and cis-OPDA homeostasis.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: This exactly matches the methods used: recombinant hydrolysis assay plus wounding metabolite profiling in single/triple mutants.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: This is a plausible inference about altered cis-OPDA release dynamics, but it is not directly tested as a tradeoff in the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Leaf wounding is the key in planta treatment used for ILR1 analysis.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper does not demonstrate direct physical protein-protein interactions between ILR1 and IAR3/ILL2/ILL6; they are only functionally related family members.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper supports redundancy among ILR1/ILL hydrolases and ILR1 being a hydrolase controlling conjugate turnover rather than a biosynthetic enzyme.
